title: agrostis brachyathera
intro: Agrostis brachyathera, also known as blue bunchgrass or blue sedge, is a type of perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Australia and New Zealand. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Agrostis brachyathera include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures for use in herbal medicine, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
